title: Steve Kerr explains how the Celtics went from an ‘afterthought’ to a ‘real favorite’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Steve Kerr praised the Celtics for becoming Eastern Conference favorites after overcoming early setbacks.
Jayson Tatum's return bolstered the Celtics, who capitalized on developing their young players during his absence.
Kerr commended the Celtics' offensive spacing, making them the league's No. 2 in offensive efficiency.

title: Advocates sue Worcester over response to mental health 911 calls, alleging discrimination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Advocates filed a federal lawsuit against Worcester, alleging discrimination in 911 responses to mental health emergencies.
The lawsuit claims Worcester police respond with armed officers to all mental health calls, escalating crises.
Fewer than 25% of Worcester police have completed crisis intervention training, according to the lawsuit.

title: Hilary Duff will be Northeastern University’s 2026 commencement speaker
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Hilary Duff announced as Northeastern University's 2026 undergraduate commencement speaker in a social media post.
Northeastern's commencement ceremony will be held at Fenway Park on April 29, 2026.
Duff is known for roles in 'Lizzie McGuire' and 'A Cinderella Story,' and her hit song with over 56 million streams.

title: 3 roster needs Patriots must still address after free-agency spending spree
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots seek a top wide receiver like A.J. Brown to boost their offense after recent free-agency moves.
New England may target edge rushers in the draft due to limited free-agent options for bolstering their defense.
The team considers drafting offensive tackle depth as current options show vulnerabilities in postseason performances.

title: Needham 16-year-old charged with murder in Roxbury slaying of woman, 61
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A 16-year-old from Needham charged with murder in the October shooting of Marie Molea-Anthony, 61, in Roxbury.
The teen faces charges of murder, aggravated assault, and unlawful possession of a firearm, while already in custody on other charges.
Police urge anyone with information on the shooting near 1855 Washington St. to contact them at 617-343-4470.

title: Chelsea man released decades after murder conviction won’t be retried, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Thomas Rosa Jr., a Chelsea man who spent 34 years in prison for murder, won't be retried, per Suffolk County DA.
New forensic evidence and changed case law led to the dropping of 1985 murder charges against Rosa.
Rosa, released in 2020, is Massachusetts' 100th exoneree since the National Registry of Exonerations began in 1989.

title: Former Attleboro man gets 35-40 years in prison for raping 5-year-old niece
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: William Machado, 35, sentenced to 35-40 years for raping a 5-year-old, followed by 10 years of probation.
Machado was convicted of multiple charges including aggravated rape of a child and assault to rape a child.
He was arrested in Chicago after months on the run and extradited to Massachusetts for trial.

title: A month after tragic shooting, Colin Dorgan, Blackstone Valley hockey triumph to win R.I. state title
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Blackstone Valley's hockey team won the Rhode Island Division II state championship, defeating Lincoln High School 3-2 in quadruple overtime.
Colin Dorgan led his team to victory, scoring the tying goal, weeks after losing family members in a tragic shooting incident.
The team wore jerseys honoring Dorgan's mother, brother, and grandfather, who were killed in the attack during the 'Senior Night' game.

title: Bruins call up former 1st-round pick Lukas Reichel on emergency basis
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins recall Lukas Reichel from Providence on emergency basis before game against Winnipeg Jets.
Reichel, a 2020 first-round pick, acquired from Vancouver for a 2026 sixth-round pick.
Reichel has five points in three AHL games; has scored 22 goals in 88 NHL career games.

title: World Cup games at Gillette no longer in jeopardy after Foxborough officially approves license
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Gillette Stadium in Foxborough secured approval to host seven World Cup games after a $7.8 million security funding agreement.
Foxborough Select Board approved the event license 5-0 after Boston Soccer 2026 pledged to cover security and staffing costs.
The event includes enhanced MBTA services, accommodating 21,000 patrons, and a traffic management plan approved by Massachusetts DOT.
